title: Mustardy X-traordinary Greek Revithia
description: A low-carb-friendly, brunch-inspired Greek dish with a tangy mustard flavor and a hint of pandan leaf, featuring a coconut cream crack topping.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the boneless chicken thighs to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together the plain Greek yogurt, Dijon mustard, and chopped fresh dill.
Add the torn pandan leaves to the bowl and stir to combine.
Place the cooked chicken thighs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the yogurt-mustard mixture evenly over the top of each chicken thigh.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken is baking, prepare the coconut cream crack topping. In a small bowl, combine the coconut cream, eggs, and a pinch of salt. Mix well until smooth.
Pour the coconut cream mixture into a large skillet or saucepan and place over medium heat.
Cook the mixture, stirring constantly, until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy sauce, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the oven and top each thigh with a spoonful of the coconut cream sauce.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve the Mustardy X-traordinary Greek Revithia hot, garnished with additional pandan leaves and fresh parsley, if desired.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together before serving.
